meta.title: The Cow and the Tiger — Set 1
meta.subject: English Reading
meta.description: Reading comprehension quiz for 'The Cow and the Tiger' — story understanding.

questions.question: What is the cow's name in the story?
questions.explanation: The cow's name is Bala. The story begins 'Once there was a cow named Bala.'

questions.question: Bala liked to graze in the shadowy forest.
questions.explanation: Yes! The story says 'Bala liked to graze in the shadowy forest and then feed her calf.'

questions.question: What happened when Bala went to the forest one day?
options: She found more grass to eat
She got lost in the forest
questions.explanation: One day, 'an angry tiger caught her.' This is the problem that starts the story.

questions.question: Bala asked the tiger to let her go so she could feed her calf.
questions.explanation: Yes! Bala said 'Please don't eat me! Let me go feed my calf.' She begged the tiger to let her go.

questions.question: What did Bala promise to do?
options: Come back to the tiger after feeding her calf
questions.explanation: Bala promised: 'I promise, I promise to come back. I'll come back soon. Then you can attack!' She kept her word.

questions.question: Bala liked to graze in the _______ forest — it was dark with tree shadows.
questions.hint: Full of shadows; dark and shaded
questions.explanation: 'Shadowy' means full of shadows and shade. The forest was dark and covered in tree shadows.

questions.question: How did the tiger feel when Bala came back as promised?
options: Angry and ready to attack immediately
Surprised and pleased that Bala kept her word
questions.explanation: 'Tiger was surprised to see her. He was pleased that Bala kept her word.' The tiger did not expect Bala to return.

questions.question: After Bala returned, the tiger still decided to eat her.
questions.explanation: The tiger said 'You told the truth. Now I won't attack.' He was so impressed by Bala's honesty that he chose NOT to eat her.

questions.question: Sort these words — are they ADJECTIVES (describing words) or VERBS (action words)?
questions.explanation: 'Beautiful,' 'shadowy,' and 'angry' describe how things look or feel (adjectives). 'Graze' and 'promise' are things you do (verbs).

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'Bala quickly fed her calf.' After the tiger let her go, Bala went home quickly to feed her baby.

questions.question: At the end of the story, the tiger promised to protect Bala from other animals.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Tiger was not mad anymore. He promised, he promised to protect Bala from other animals.' The tiger became her protector!

questions.question: What does 'graze' mean?
options: To eat grass in a field or forest
questions.explanation: 'Graze' means to eat grass. Bala liked to graze — eat grass — in the shadowy forest.

questions.question: Bala said she would keep her _______. She came back just as she said.
questions.explanation: A 'promise' is a commitment to do something. Bala kept her promise by returning to the tiger as she said she would.

questions.question: What is the moral of 'The Cow and the Tiger'?
options: Always keep your promises and tell the truth
questions.explanation: The moral stated at the end of the story is: 'Always keep your promises. Always tell the truth.' Bala's honesty changed the tiger's heart.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form the moral of the story:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'Always keep your promises.' This is the first part of the story's moral at the very end.
